items.title: Can states cap fuel prices? Tasmanian emergency fuel laws explained

children.content: The Tasmanian government is seeking to have the power to cap petrol prices. But how will it work? And if it does, can other states do the same?

children.content: A bill to give the Tasmanian government power to cap fuel prices has passed the state's parliament after a late-night sitting.

items.title: Rabid robot dogs, a floating art gallery and exclusive film at Dark Mofo 2026

children.content: The new spirit of Tasmania ferries might not be available for journeys across the Bass Strait yet — but this winter, one of them will take on a new role as an art gallery, during Hobart's Dark Mofo festival.

items.title: Justice department confirms not guilty plea by ex-National Trust Tasmania boss on theft charges

children.content: Following the lifting of a suppression order, Tasmania's department of justice has confirmed the former director of the National Trust in Tasmania has pleaded not guilty to stealing almost $150,000 worth of items from his former employer.

items.title: Aboriginal woman seeks on-country court hearing for trespass case

children.content: A member of the Tasmanian Aboriginal community charged with trespass has applied to the Hobart Magistrates Court to have her case heard on Aboriginal-owned land.

image.altText: People gathered outside court with an Aboriginal flag

items.title: TassieCast: The manosphere creeping into Tassie classrooms

children.content: The manosphere is reaching into Tassie classrooms, fuelling misogyny and anti-social behaviour.
How and why are men, particular young men, getting caught up in it web?

items.title: This man went viral after surviving Bondi. Then the internet took a dark turn

children.content: Arsen Ostrovsky was on the ground, bleeding, at the Bondi attack when he decided to take a selfie. Within 2 hours it was circulating on the internet. In less than a day, it had become fodder for a global conspiracy theory.

items.title: Dementia is Australia's top killer, yet most still miss the early signs

image.altText: A close-up shot of a hand resting on an elderly woman's hands.

children.content: Dementia is the leading cause of death in Australia and stigmas around the disease can delay diagnosis. But some in the community are at work to break taboos.

items.title: 'Built on deception': How one online travel site is fleecing customers

children.content: As holiday-makers gear up for Easter, online travel site Guest Reservations is accused of fleecing customers using hidden fees and charges with advocates calling on government and the regulator to step in.

items.title: Do we actually have the workforce for the $4b NDIS alternative?

children.content: Months away from the start of a huge shift in disability support for children, the workforce expected to deliver it has concerns about its readiness.

items.title: Ranked number one on the terrorism index, this nation struck back

items.title: Head of TGA speaks out on sunscreen scandal

children.content: Recently, online discussion has turned to what some perceive to be a spate of influencers getting injured.

items.title: A century ago, a Liberal Party disintegrated, and there are lessons for its namesake

children.content: Last week's South Australian election has left the Liberals in the grip of what one analyst described as an "existential crisis". As the Liberals seek to learn the lessons of the past, they should bear in mind the example of their once mighty namesakes, who ruled Britain for decades before their party turned to dust.

items.title: Savannah Guthrie to return to Today show after mother's disappearance

children.content: Savannah Guthrie is scheduled to return to her job as a co-anchor of NBC's Today show on April 6, more than two months after her 84-year-old mother's unresolved abduction.

image.altText: Savannah Guthrie (left) with her mum (right).

items.title: Blueberry industry under scrutiny amid rapid growth

children.content: Australians are eating and growing more blueberries than ever before, but questions are being asked about how production of the fruit is being regulated.

items.title: End of an era for general store owned by family for 100 years

children.content: The Tharwa General Store — which has been run by the same family since 1926 — has been many things: a post office, a mechanic's workshop, a fuel stop, a lolly shop, a watering hole, and above all, a place where a community comes together.

image.altText: A black and white image of a woman reaching for something behind the counter of a regional general store.

items.title: Bush artist 'Pro' Hart remembered 20 years after death

children.content: On March 28, 2006, Broken Hill lost one of its most renowned residents in internationally acclaimed artist Kevin "Pro" Hart. He was, and still is, considered a widely popular artist, but his recognition was divisive to different audiences.

items.title: Signs point to measles cases spreading undetected in NSW

children.content: An outbreak of measles in south-east Asia has fuelled a surge in NSW cases — but the state's biggest cause for concern is five infections believed to have been spread undetected in the community.

items.title: Liberals to retain SA opposition status despite One Nation's second seat, ABC projects
